description: Expanded golden retrieval dataset covering policy/technical/numeric/multi-hop categories.  Each item ingests a text document with multiple sections; the harness asserts that the chunk containing `expected_substring` appears in the top-K retrieval.

sections: All employees are entitled to 20 paid vacation days per calendar year. Unused vacation days may be carried over up to a maximum of 5 days into the next year.
Remote work is permitted up to 3 days per week with manager approval. Employees working remotely must maintain a secure home office environment and use a company-issued VPN.
Expense reimbursements are processed within 10 business days of submission. Receipts must be uploaded via the Expensify integration within 30 days of the expense date.
Parental leave provides 16 weeks of paid time off for primary caregivers and 8 weeks for secondary caregivers, effective from the child's birth or adoption placement.
The security incident response team must be notified within 1 hour of any suspected data breach via the #sec-incidents Slack channel or a page to the on-call rotation.
Professional development budget is $2,500 per employee per year, covering courses, books, and conference attendance. Pre-approval is required for any single expense over $500.
The staging environment runs on a Kubernetes cluster in us-east-1, provisioned via Terraform in the `infra/staging` module. Node pools auto-scale between 3 and 12 nodes.
Postgres backups run nightly at 02:00 UTC using pg_dump, with a 30-day retention policy. Point-in-time recovery is available for the most recent 7 days via continuous WAL archiving.
The ingestion worker reads jobs from the `document_jobs` table using a SKIP LOCKED row-level lock. Heartbeats must refresh every 60 seconds or the job is considered orphaned.
Redis is used only as a cache for session tokens and rate-limit counters. Any data that must survive a restart should live in Postgres, not Redis.
pgvector HNSW indexes should be rebuilt after bulk ingests of more than 10,000 chunks. Use `REINDEX INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_document_chunks_embedding_hnsw` to avoid downtime.

The CI pipeline runs backend pytest + frontend `npm run build` + `pytest -m eval` in parallel. The eval job compares `docs/eval/last_run.json` against `docs/eval/baseline.json` and fails the build if recall drops by more than 2 percentage points.
Large language models fine-tuned on domain-specific corpora show a 14.7% absolute improvement on downstream QA benchmarks compared to zero-shot baselines, measured across 12 tasks.
Reciprocal rank fusion with k=60 produced the best overall retrieval quality in our ablation study, outperforming linear-weighted score fusion by 3.2 points on mean recall@10.
Contextual retrieval, which prepends a one-sentence document-level summary to each chunk before embedding, reduced retrieval failures by up to 49% in the evaluations we conducted.
Cross-encoder rerankers added 120-280ms of latency at p95 but improved MRR by 8-15% depending on corpus. We recommend gating rerank behind a feature flag for latency-sensitive paths.
Graph-based retrieval complemented dense retrieval for multi-hop questions requiring synthesis across 3+ documents, but provided no benefit for single-document factoid questions.

items.question: How many paid vacation days per year do employees get?
Can I carry unused vacation into next year?
items.expected_substring: carried over up to a maximum of 5 days
